headline: Ukraine emergency re-queue confirmed: Russia broke Kyiv-declared unilateral ceasefire within hours of midnight start with 108 combat drones and 3 missiles, killing 1 civilian and wounding 3 in Sumy region. Russian-Ukrainian competing-ceasefire framework failed at first operational test. Per methodology v1.2 bad-faith-ceasefire rule, the violation pattern is an inverted signal toward Russia (counterparty), NOT toward Ukraine. Ukraine's proactive humanitarian-window declaration plus its restrained public framing (FM Sybiha: 'Putin only cares about military parades, not human lives') is signal-positive on AWR/INT but composite-neutral pending sustained behavior. Composite 50.0 (published) → 50.0 (sustained). Sub-threshold delta. Functional band sustained. Russia ceasefire violation strengthens documentation against Russia (counter-party) for future cycle.
key_evidence: May 6 2026: Russia launched 108 combat drones and 3 missiles within hours of Ukraine's midnight ceasefire commencement, killing 1 civilian (Sumy region) and wounding 3 (Euronews, France 24, Washington Post May 6).

Russian Defense Ministry counter-claimed Ukraine struck Russian regions/Crimea with 53 drones overnight; Crimea regional head reported 5 civilian deaths from Ukrainian strikes — both sides claim violation, but Russian violation occurred during Ukraine's own declared cessation window.
FM Andrii Sybiha public framing: 'Putin only cares about military parades, not human lives' — measured, civilian-harm-centered framing rather than escalatory rhetoric.

Per methodology v1.2 bad-faith-ceasefire rule: declared cessation concurrent with documented violations is an inverted signal toward the violating party. Russia, not Ukraine, is the inverted-signal counterparty.
Cumulative wartime governance evidence base sustained: UN HRMMU monitoring; Ombudsperson active; HRW 2026 World Report; civil-society engagement; humanitarian corridor proposals.
Sub-threshold positive AWR/INT signal from Ukraine's continued humanitarian-window posture; not yet operationally proven over multi-day window.

evidence.finding: Russia broke unilateral ceasefire with drone and missile attacks; Ukrainian FM confirmed 108 combat drones and 3 missiles.
evidence.polarity: positive (re Ukraine framing); negative-counterparty (re Russia violation)

evidence.finding: Russian drone attack on civilian car in Sumy killed passenger, wounded driver; Ukrainian drone strikes also reported in Crimea (5 civilians).
Russia snubs Ukraine's unilateral ceasefire, firing dozens of drones; counter-claims of Ukrainian strikes on Russian regions.
Ukraine officially says Russia violated ceasefire initiated by Kyiv; cumulative documentation.
Russia violated ceasefire 'within minutes' framing — speed of violation reinforces bad-faith-ceasefire pattern documentation.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 3.0 unchanged. Ukraine's humanitarian-window declaration is a sustained AWR-positive signal (proactive recognition of civilian-suffering reduction opportunity), but it has not yet held over multi-day window and so does not move the dimension.
scoring_rationale.BND: 3.0 unchanged. Russia's violation occurred during Ukraine's declared cessation — counterparty boundary failure, not Ukraine's. Ukraine's restraint in framing (FM Sybiha statement) is a modest BND-positive indicator.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 3.0 unchanged. No structural-governance change in window.
scoring_rationale.INT: 3.0 unchanged. Sustained humanitarian-window posture combined with measured public framing under attack provocation is INT-positive at signal level; awaiting longitudinal verification before composite movement.

math_check.validator_check: No change. Sub-threshold; confirmation only. May 9-10 next verification window.

negativeSignals: Civilian deaths (1 in Sumy from Russian drone) during Ukraine's own ceasefire window; ongoing wartime conditions; Crimea civilian deaths from Ukrainian strikes (counter-claim).
positiveSignals: Proactive humanitarian-window declaration; restrained public framing of violation by FM; sustained civil-society and Ombudsperson engagement; willingness to act independently of adversary framing.
sector_context: Ukraine ceasefire violation by Russia confirmed within hours. May 9-10 emergency re-queue (post-Russian Victory Day) verifies whether the second ceasefire window holds. Russia counter-party documentation strengthened. Ukraine's INT/AWR signals sustained.
open_actions_resolved: May 6 ceasefire violation by Russia documented
Bad-faith-ceasefire-rule application: counterparty inversion (Russia, not Ukraine) confirmed
Ukraine FM framing tracked as INT signal
open_actions_carried: May 9-10 emergency re-queue: verify whether either ceasefire produced sustained cessation
Cross-reference Russia entity for counter-party scoring documentation
Track Ukraine humanitarian-window posture longitudinally for AWR/INT composite movement
